quote: I have been using Vice Pro and Vice Pro Plus golf balls since 2019. I have found them to be very consistent, they feel great, they spin, and they fly just as far as either the Pro V1 or the TP5s that I was playing, for a much better price.
Love Vice Pro balls. I'm a 7 handicap and have played all the brand balls. The Vice Pro matches or beats them all and at a great price.
I played T balls for years but switched to Vice after finding a ball and playing it (Vice Pro plus). What a great ball! Spin, feel, distance - all comparable to premium balls at half the price.
Love i mean I absolutely love the tracer Pro Vice ball. It has everything the ProV1 has but it's almost half the price. I bought 6 dozen and it was such a great price I will never go back.
They are awesome, won't go back to any other premium brand...
I'm fully converted. Similar performance at almost half the price when buying in bulk.
source: Golf Equipment Hub - Vice Pro Review

quote: I could never justify playing them. I could easily lose 3 or 4 balls a round at my old course, so the cost, and my lack of talent combined to make them way too expensive.
source: Golf Monthly Forum - Pro V1 Discussion

quote: Only if you're happy with the cost of each ball you lose. I still lose a few balls every round so it's not worth it for me to head into the premium bracket. The likes of AD333 and Supersofts are under half the price of ProV1s, which is a crazy difference.
source: Golf Monthly Forum - Pro V1 Discussion

quote: Love Pro V1s, but would never ever buy them. Fortunately I have PPs who give them to me when they find them.
source: Golf Monthly Forum - Pro V1 Discussion

quote: I've switched from TP5's to Snell MTB black, half the price and I noticed no difference at all. I like the Pro V but until I'm on tour, I won't be wasting my money on them.
source: Golf Monthly Forum - Pro V1 Discussion

quote: Been playing Pro V's for years, discovered the Snells about 18 months ago. Played the MTB black for a year loved them. When the Reds came out I was completely sold on them went from a 14.3 to a 12.7 in one summer. Can't say it was all the new Reds, but they did make a BIG difference 100 yards and in. And the price is the best part for an old retired guy.
I'm sorry, but it is simple. If you are a golfer who wants the performance of a premium golf ball and NOT at a premium price, then play Snell MTB golf balls, period.

I have been using Snell golf balls for the last 2 years. They are as good or better than any premium golf ball that I have played. The spin on these golf balls is unbelievable with short irons. Do yourself a favor and try a dozen, you won't go back to your old golf ball!!

A friend of mine brought some to our annual golf trip, so I thought what the heck. I may never switch back. If the Snell ball and the Pro-V ball were the same price, I would still take the Snell ball.

My 13 year old son is ranked #1 in our state for his age and he has always been a Pro-V1x ball snob. I gave him a sleeve to try out. He called me from the course and asked me to get him more. We buy them by the 6 dozen value deal.

I thought if I could hit a ball as good as a PROV1 at a reduced cost it would be worth trying. So I gave them to three of my golf buddies that use strictly PORV1's. They now use Snell's. All three said that the Snell was a better ball. It stayed in the air longer, which means it traveled further.

I have used the Titleist PROV1 ball for many years, but lately, the price is skyrocketing. I ordered the MTB Red ball early this summer just to try the comparison. It's softer, has plenty of bite on the chip shots and the greens. WELL WORTH THE MONEY HERE! and I love the yellows.

I tried your Snell MTB black recently and enjoyed it very much. I found the distance equal to ProV1 at $16 less a dozen. I just ordered two more dozen and have become quite a Snell Ambassador at my golf club.
I notice no difference between the Snell and pro V1X I play; except the cost that is. Great ball, nice price; sounds like a winner.
Love the Snell ball. Been playing it for the last 2 seasons. Great ball and even better price. Can't justify the ProV1 cost for the quality of my game.

I have been playing the MTB black since it's been out... like it better over all than any ball I've ever played. My index is 4. If I could buy any ball at the same price, I would still prefer the black.
MTB-X IS A GREAT BALL. PLAYED THE MTB RED BEFORE, BUT THE X BALL SURPASSED MY EXPECTATIONS. OUTSTANDING BALL FOR HALF THE PRICE OF A TITLEIST.

Just over a year a friend recommended the Snell Golf Balls to me. Being at the time a ProV1 acolyte, I was somewhat skeptical. My friend, however, is a player and he was very pleased with Snell's performance. Well, he was right! I've been using MTB-Black for nearly a year now and couldn't be more pleased. I've been recommending Snell to anyone I play with.
I recently compared over a dozen balls and have ended up committing to the Vice Pro for 2023.

And amazingly to me, the Vice Pro came out the winner... In a head to head matchup with the Pro V1 over like 100 swings - The Vice Pro launched higher, spun more, and went further with every club. It was an absolute slaughter. This is a very legit ball. For the price IMO it's the best ball on the market.
I was a devout Titleist player but am now a Vice convert. I can't comprehend why anyone would spend almost $4 a ball for Titleist Pro V1 or Pro V1x.
source: MyGolfSpy - Vice Pro Plus vs Pro V1x

quote: I formally played Pro V1X's or Pro V1's and can't tell the difference, except price.
source: MyGolfSpy - Vice Pro Plus vs Pro V1x

quote: Over the last two seasons I have switched over to Vice. Previously, I used Mizuno RB Tour and Bridgestone balls, making the switch was an easy choice. Not only is VICE extremely affordable there are options for every hitter. The PRO Zero might be the best all around ball I've used. Durable, good control, good spin, flies off the club face.
The product was as described. Great Ball for the cost. Careful there is quite a difference between the Vice Pro and the Vice Pro Plus.
The ball itself is fine. Big fan of the Pro Plus. The problem is the color. Blends in with everything. Impossible to find off the fairway.
source: Amazon Review - Vice Pro Plus Gold

quote: Went to a Titleist ball fitting at my club in Tampa in December mostly because they gave you a dozen of the not yet released Pro-V1x's for attending. After hearing about Mr. Snell's history and doing some reading and YouTube watching, I bought 2 dozen MTB Red. After using all of the new free Titleist balls, I opened the first box this week. The MTB's fly like crazy, hold greens incredibly well even on longer approach shots, and roll off the putter with great feel. They are also very durable.
Since I'll be playing Snell balls from now on, I don't know what I'll do with all of my Titleist hats!

I recently returned to Arizona from Canada and needed balls so I ordered a split dozen of MTB Black and MTB red in yellow. After playing both I decided on buying 2 dozen more of the MTB Black. I have gained almost all the distance I have lost over the last 8-9 years. At 71 years of age I'm back to hitting it 240 to 250. What a pleasant surprise!

I jumped on the Snell bandwagon in 2015 when they first came out and immediately loved playing these balls. I did my research and confirmed the performance on the course. Bought a hat and became a self-appointed Snell evangelist in the Philadelphia area.

Honestly at this point I choose Snell over everything else because I like Dean and what he's done for industry so much. The balls just flat perform, and I tee it up with pride, there's something badass about playing a Snell, you know? MTBx works best for me but honestly they're both great, top tier balls, can't really go wrong.
I just picked up a dozen of the new MTBX I absolutely love the feel of the golf ball off the driver and putting I am sold on this ball it goes real far I just wish I would've bought them sooner.
Fantastic quality balls at a fair price. Best value available! Highly recommend! From a 9 handicap (if that matters).

The balls and gloves received feel premium and are much more fun color wise than traditional products on the market. Seem comparable to Titleist products that cost much more and haven't innovated in years.

I am really enjoying the Vice Pro golf balls. I am getting the same or better distance with a nice soft feel. Performance around the green is also exceptional. I can get up to four rounds a ball if I play well.

Ordering golf products from Vice Golf is easy, simple and well worth the money! They are the only golf ball I have been using for 7 years. They feel better off the club, flight is true and green performance is excellent.

The Vice Pro golf balls have completely won me over. I love that they come in different designs, it's refreshing to see some creativity in a market full of plain white balls. Performance-wise, they deliver.
Price exceptional and quick turn around. I got the Pro Air in white. I can honestly say the Air has been best ball at my skill 13 HC, and I've used everything under the sun.
Tried the Pro ball for the price, and they just click with the way I play. Shot my 2 lowest rounds back to back in my first 2 rounds playing them.
Very impressed with this ball! Budget friendly at $30 a box of 12. It might be better than Pro V1's. If you use Pro V1's you should try this ball.
These new ones with the hollow arrow test out about the same as Pro V1's. At $27.99 for 2 dozen not $49 for 1 dozen.
I think the ProV1 is overpriced trash. The cover still wears like crap, even after they redesigned it. Plus, with all the balls Titleist produces, quality control has gone downhill.
Not saying I dislike Pro Vs but they are just too expensive for my game.

For the price, you can't beat the latest version of the Kirkland Signature ball. It might not be the best ball on the market, but we saw little difference between the Kirkland Signature and balls that cost two or three times as much.
I couldn't tell the difference between them when putting blindfolded against other urethane-covered balls.
In my testing, the Kirkland and Pro V1 were nearly identical in feel. The Kirkland might be slightly firmer but nothing dramatic. For the price difference, it's a no-brainer.
source: Out of Bounds Golf - Kirkland Review

quote: As good or better than Pro V1. If they have them when I go this week I will be buying.
source: MyGolfSpy - Kirkland vs Pro V1 Comments

quote: The new golf balls are way too expensive. This is the best deal around.
source: Best Buy Review - Refurbished Pro V1

quote: Pro V ones are the best golf ball out there but underrated because they're so overpriced.

Playing one day with a Pro V1 looking for a lost ball found a Snell MTB Black new ball. I tried it off the next tee and had my first birdie of the day. Played with it the next 14 holes had 3 more birdies. Still have the ball.
Greg Richard: States the MTB-X is a great ball. Every bit as good as a Pro V or Taylormade TP. I LOVE the price as well.
Never again will I pay premium prices for a premium ball. I'm a Snell guy now.
I have just purchased 9 dozen of the black great ball. Great company to deal with.
For average golfer, the Snell balls make a lot of sense, save money without any performance or feel sacrifice.

I am very happy with the results and will look forward to trying the MTB X balls in future purchase. Thank you for your efforts in giving consumer costs consideration, while maintaining quality product.
The Pro V1 balls are not worth $55 a dozen if you are not a good enough golfer to appreciate the difference, or if you are on an exceptionally tight budget.
